title: Box Breathing
blurb: Equal counts to steady the mind under pressure.
lead: Box breathing uses equal counts of inhale, hold, exhale, and hold — a simple way to steady the nervous system, used even by high-pressure professionals.

blocks.text: Box breathing (or square breathing) means breathing in four equal phases: inhale, hold, exhale, hold — each for the same count.
The rhythm and gentle breath-holds promote a sense of control and calm. It's widely used for stress and focus; evidence is promising and developing.

items: Inhale through your nose for a count of 4.
Hold empty for 4. Repeat for several rounds.

items.label: Best for
items.value: Stress, focus, staying calm under pressure

blocks.text: Straining during holds. Keep counts comfortable — shorten to 3 if 4 feels tense.

resources.title: MindShift CBT
resources.whyHere: Free app with guided calm-breathing tools.
